rationale: The citing paper uses Chandy et al. (2006) in the core problem framing that innovation depends not just on generating inputs but on converting those inputs into realized innovation outcomes. That conversion challenge is then central to the paper's theorizing about project coordination and task configuration. Although the citing paper does not reuse the cited paper's specific empirical design or detailed predictors, the citation plays a meaningful conceptual role beyond a generic literature list mention.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on the cited paper's conversion perspective to motivate its own theory of how project governance (especially general contractor task participation) helps or hinders turning project inputs into innovation outcomes. In this sense, it uses the cited work as conceptual grounding for its coordination-focused framework.
